senses.definition: to make someone look like a blackguard; to act in a way that discredits or damages someone’s reputation by calling them a scoundrel.
examples: They accused him of trying to blackguardize their leader during the debate.
The article blackguardized the journalist, suggesting he was biased.

senses.definition: acting like a blackguard; dishonorable or mean-spirited.
examples: His blackguardly tricks won him no friends.
They used blackguardly tactics in the campaign.

senses.definition: the behavior or quality of a blackguard; contemptible conduct.
examples: The speech was full of blackguardry and deceit.
He rejected their blackguardry as petty lies.

senses.definition: men or people who behave dishonorably; villains or scoundrels.
examples: The town was distressed by the actions of a few blackguards.
Only a few blackguards were left in the crowd.

senses.definition: a type of tree or its wood, often with dark resin-rich heartwood; also refers to the tree or its fruit in some regions.
examples: The blackgum tree provides durable wood for furniture.
In the swamp, blackgum trees line the water edge.

senses.definition: plural of blackgum; multiple trees or the wood/fruit of blackgum.
examples: Dense patches of blackgums shaded the old trail.
Blackgums are tapped for resin in some regions.

senses.definition: a person who uses or belongs to a blackhand organization or uses intimidation; a violent thug.
examples: The police tracked a blackhander involved in the racket.
The gang included several notorious blackhanders.

senses.definition: plural of blackhander; multiple members of a blackhand or similar group.
examples: The gang had several feared blackhanders.
Witnesses exposed two blackhanders during the raid.

senses.definition: a clogged pore on the skin near the surface; a common acne lesion.
examples: She squeezed a blackhead with clean fingers.
Regular cleansing helps prevent blackheads.

senses.definition: having a black head or dark-colored head; used in biology or describing organisms with dark heads.
examples: The blackheaded gull is common along the coast.
We studied the blackheaded larvae under the microscope.

senses.definition: plural of blackhead; multiple clogged pores on the skin.
examples: She tried to remove several blackheads from her face.
Different formulas help treat blackheads.

senses.definition: a person considered merciless or cruel; a villain at heart.
examples: He was a blackheart who betrayed friends for gain.
The villain in the story is a true blackheart.

senses.definition: having or showing a cruel, unkind, or merciless heart.
examples: Her blackhearted remarks wounded many people.
He gave a blackhearted smile.

senses.definition: in a cruel or mean-spirited way.
examples: He spoke blackheartedly about his rival.
They grinned blackheartedly at the misfortune.

senses.definition: the quality of being cruel or mean-spirited.
examples: The novel explores themes of blackheartedness in politics.
Her blackheartedness shocked the community.

senses.definition: people regarded as cruel or unscrupulous; plural of blackheart.
examples: The group of blackhearts plotted in secrecy.
She refused to associate with the blackhearts in town.

senses.definition: a slang or informal term that refers to a Black person; can be considered offensive; also a nickname for a person named Blackie.
examples: The term blackie can be offensive; use with caution.
They named the stray dog Blackie.

senses.definition: plural form of blackie; may refer to people or nicknames; can be offensive in some contexts.
examples: The group of blackies we met at the market were friendly.
Some old songs use the term blackies in a dated way.

senses.definition: the act of applying black substance to something; also a substance used for polishing or for soot.
examples: The sailor was rubbing blacking onto the boots.
During the play, the stagehands used blacking to darken areas.

senses.definition: plural of blacking; substances or tasks involving applying blacking.
examples: They bought several canisters of blacKings for the stage production.
Different brands of blackings exist for boots.

senses.definition: somewhat black in color; dark or slightly shaded.
examples: Her hair was blackish brown in the sunlight.
The water looked blackish in the night.

senses.definition: in a dark or blackish manner; somewhat darkly.
examples: The room looked blackishly lit by the lone lamp.
He spoke blackishly, shading his words with suspicion.

senses.definition: the quality of being blackish or dark.
examples: The ink gave the paper a look of blackishness.
They noted the blackishness of the soil after rain.

senses.definition: informal or dialectal term that may refer to a person or thing associated with black; context dependent; may be offensive.
examples: The term blackit appeared in the early diary as a descriptor.
Avoid using blackit in modern writing.

senses.definition: a gambling card game played against a dealer; also a term for a concealed weapon or a weapon; also a chair in some dialect.
examples: In casinos, blackjack is a popular card game.
He dealt his hand in blackjack and won big.

senses.definition: slang for a weapon, especially a blackjack (club).
examples: The thug grabbed a blackjack from under his coat.
Security found a blackjack in his bag.
